event: The Martyrdom of Saint Agnes in the Roman Forum
symbols: vast Roman architecture — the overwhelming power of the state that the young martyr defied
massive crowd — the public spectacle of persecution, meant to intimidate but ultimately inspiring
temples to pagan gods — the idolatrous system that Agnes refused to worship
description: A monumental panoramic view of the Roman Forum packed with architecture and human figures. Grand classical buildings, colonnaded temples, triumphal arches, and towering statues fill the scene from edge to edge. The architecture rises in tiers up a hill toward grand temples at the summit. In the lower foreground and middle ground, enormous crowds gather on steps and in open spaces — thousands of tiny figures. The scene is suffused with warm golden light under a partly cloudy sky.

_meta.title: The Martyrdom of St. Agnes in the Roman Forum
